hero.name: Digital Designer creating brands and experiences that convert.

about.title: About
about.heading: I am David Kim, a Digital Designer specialized in building consistent visual presence from branding to digital products.
about.description: I work across branding, visual identity, UX/UI, social media, and web design, connecting strategy and execution to build memorable brands and efficient interfaces.

summary.title: About / Professional Summary
summary.text: UX/UI and Product Designer with experience in digital design and web and mobile product development. Working as a digital designer since 2021, creating interfaces, visual identities, and digital solutions for a range of clients and projects. Also has experience developing web and mobile applications, with front-end, back-end, and database knowledge, enabling end-to-end participation in digital product creation, from concept to delivery. Focused on creating intuitive, scalable, and visually consistent digital experiences by connecting design, technology, and product strategy.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Projects organized by specialty to show both depth and range across digital design work.

list.name: BuddyTech
list.problem: Sales teams lose time on low-potential leads due to lack of smart prioritization based on interaction data.
list.process: We mapped the sales journey, prioritized high-impact MVP features, and validated a straightforward interface to support faster decision-making.
list.solution: We built an AI Sales Buddy that analyzes interactions, calculates lead score, and recommends strategic next steps for each opportunity.
list.result: Winning MVP at the +Devs2Blu Hackathon by ACATE, recognized for problem clarity, real-world applicability, and conversion impact potential.
list.overview: BuddyTech is an intelligent sales assistant MVP built during the +Devs2Blu Hackathon by ACATE. The product was designed to analyze customer interactions and help sellers prioritize leads with higher conversion potential.
list.challenge: Build a functional AI Sales Assistant MVP capable of combining interaction signals (emails, meetings, notes, and CRM data) with historical wins to generate lead scoring and strategic next-step recommendations.
roleItems: Continuous alignment between design and development
strategyItems: Simplicity: keep focus on lead prioritization
User experience: clear guidance on what to do and why
Data-driven intelligence: recommendations based on conversion patterns
learningsItems: Leadership under high-speed constraints
Product prioritization focused on business value
Fast decision-making in innovation environments
Connection between UX, data, and commercial outcomes

list.name: Evolution
list.problem: People with clear goals struggle to maintain consistency over time without proper tracking and motivational feedback.
list.process: We identified core user pain points, structured a gamification approach based on XP and levels, and organized the MVP around usability and daily motivation.
list.solution: A mobile app with a gamified habit system: task logging, XP points, level progression, consistency streaks, and visual progress tracking.
list.result: Positive evaluation from the course panel, highlighted for the topic's relevance, clarity of the gamification concept, and simple yet functional design.
list.overview: Evolution is a mobile app developed to help people build consistency in their habits and personal goals. The solution transforms daily objectives into gamified tasks, allowing users to track their progress through XP, levels, and visual evolution metrics.
list.challenge: Build a functional mobile MVP in Flutter that made habit tracking motivating and consistent — combining gamification mechanics with visual progress metrics to overcome lack of discipline and difficulty perceiving real growth.
strategyItems: Gamification: turn tasks into achievements with XP, levels, and streaks
Progress visualization: clear and motivating graphs and visual indicators
Motivation and purpose: integration with inspirational content and daily quotes
learningsItems: Team leadership in digital product projects
Mobile app design focused on user behavior
Applying gamification as a UX strategy
Building behavior-focused MVPs

list.name: Uxtreme
list.problem: Many digital products fail in usability because they do not follow a complete UX process, leading to confusing flows, low accessibility, and poorly validated interfaces.
list.process: We applied a full UX/UI workflow: problem discovery, experience structuring (user flows and architecture), low-fidelity wireframes, mid and high-fidelity prototypes, plus usability and accessibility validation.
list.solution: We built a user-centered digital solution with an end-to-end prototype, product design methodology, and strong focus on clarity, intuitive navigation, and visual consistency.
list.result: Uxtreme consolidated all Entra21 UX/UI methodologies in practice, demonstrating full-process execution, product thinking, and real application of usability and accessibility best practices in Figma.
list.overview: Uxtreme was developed in 2024 as the final project of the Entra21 UX/UI program, combining all methods and practices learned during the course. The project goes through every product design stage, from problem discovery to high-fidelity prototyping, focused on UX/UI process in Figma (without code implementation).
list.challenge: Build a complete user-centered digital solution while applying market-standard methodology across all phases: research, problem definition, experience structure, prototyping, validation, and accessibility.
roleItems: User flow and information architecture design
strategyItems: Discovery: research, problem framing, persona, and benchmarking
Ideation: HMW, moodboard, and UX Writing
Experience structuring: user journey, information architecture, user flow, and wireflow
Prototyping and validation: wireframes, forms, and usability testing
Collaborative channel-based organization to document design decisions
learningsItems: End-to-end digital experience structuring
From wireframes to high-fidelity prototypes
User-centered product thinking
Practical accessibility and usability execution
Design process organization in team environments

list.name: Timeroom
list.problem: Users and teams lost time managing schedules and booking slots through confusing workflows with low visibility.
list.process: We investigated booking journey steps, mapped recurring conflicts, and simplified the flow into a focused journey with real-time feedback.
list.solution: We designed Timeroom with intelligent scheduling, clear availability visualization, and fast time-slot confirmation.
list.result: Lower friction in booking flows, fewer scheduling conflicts, and a smoother experience for end users.
list.overview: Timeroom is a digital product concept created to optimize time management and reservations with an intuitive interface, prioritizing clarity and speed in decision-making.
list.challenge: Turn a naturally complex scheduling process into a simple, reliable, and easy-to-use experience across devices.
strategyItems: Few-step booking flow
Immediate visibility of available time slots
Visual feedback to reduce mistakes and rework
learningsItems: Simplicity is crucial for scheduling products
Micro-interactions reduce user uncertainty
Availability clarity prevents operational conflicts
Well-structured UX improves business efficiency

list.name: Forum Barbearia Rebranding
list.problem: Forum Barbearia had strong local recognition but lacked technical and visual consistency to scale communication across social media, storefront signage, uniforms, and print materials.
list.process: We executed a full rebranding workflow: brand diagnosis, positioning attribute definition, visual concept exploration, typography and symbol refinement, legibility testing at multiple sizes, and consolidation of an application-ready identity system.
list.solution: Logo rebranding with a redesigned core mark, improved proportions, contrast and readability, horizontal and reduced lockups, supporting color palette, typographic standards, and practical usage guidelines for digital and physical media.
list.result: The new identity increased the brand's professional perception, unified communication across touchpoints, and made asset production faster and more consistent.
list.overview: A complete branding update for Forum Barbearia focused on logo evolution and visual language refinement to better express a modern, trustworthy, and premium barbershop positioning. Scope included diagnosis, strategy, redesign, and implementation guidance.
list.challenge: Modernize the brand without losing existing customer recognition, balancing personality with technical clarity and cross-channel adaptability.
roleItems: Creative direction and rebranding concept definition
Visual system structuring for practical applications
Usage guideline organization for long-term consistency
strategyItems: Preserve brand recognition while evolving visual quality
Prioritize readability and contrast at small sizes
Build a flexible system for digital, signage, and print
Standardize application rules to avoid distortions
learningsItems: Effective rebranding balances continuity and change
Technical logo quality directly impacts perceived brand value
A clear application system reduces production errors and rework
Visual consistency strengthens positioning and brand memory

list.name: Catedral do Avivamento Rebranding
list.problem: The previous identity of Catedral do Avivamento did not clearly communicate its institutional message and showed inconsistent usage across ministries, social channels, and support materials.
list.process: The rebranding followed a structured flow: current identity diagnosis, definition of core attributes (welcome, faith, and authority), visual exploration, typographic and symbolic refinement, legibility validation at multiple scales, and consolidation of practical usage rules.
list.solution: We developed a new logo system with improved balance, readability, and reproduction quality. The system includes primary and reduced lockups, spacing guidance, alignment rules, and recommendations for digital and print use.
list.result: The new identity strengthened Catedral do Avivamento's institutional presence, improved visual consistency across materials, and made communication production more efficient and reliable.
list.overview: A logo rebranding project for Catedral do Avivamento focused on translating spiritual and institutional values into a contemporary, consistent, and versatile visual signature.
list.challenge: Modernize the brand without breaking community recognition, balancing symbolism, sobriety, and technical clarity for different reproduction contexts.
roleItems: Audit and analysis of the existing identity
Rebranding concept and visual direction definition
Technical tuning for readability and reproduction
strategyItems: Preserve essential symbolic elements of the brand
Increase readability and contrast at every size
Standardize usage to reduce inconsistencies
Build a scalable visual base for institutional communication
learningsItems: Institutional projects require balance between heritage and renewal
Brand consistency depends on clear application rules
Technical logo details directly affect credibility perception
Well-defined visual direction speeds up asset production

list.name: Launch Week Social Campaign
list.problem: Low engagement and campaign pieces disconnected from the offer narrative.
list.solution: Created a campaign-driven visual system with modular templates and staged storytelling.
list.result: Higher visual consistency, better reach performance, and improved engagement rates.

list.name: IBK Institutional Website
list.problem: Previous website had weak structure, poor readability, and unclear navigation.
list.solution: Redesigned core pages with stronger visual hierarchy and improved information clarity.
list.result: More fluid navigation and a more professional institutional presence.

process.title: Process
process.description: A clear framework that turns business goals into strong visual and digital solutions.

steps.title: Research
steps.description: Understand brand context, audience, and business goals.

steps.title: Strategy
steps.description: Define direction, priorities, and visual opportunities.

steps.title: Concept
steps.description: Build concepts aligned with positioning and usability.

steps.title: Design
steps.description: Develop high-quality visual assets and interface systems.

steps.title: Delivery
steps.description: Finalize files, guidelines, and implementation support.
